reasoning: The question asks whether the US national deposit rate for 12-month CDs will have strictly increased by 2025-12-09 and 2026-02-07 compared to its baseline on 2025-11-09. Over the 2024-2025 period, the macroeconomic environment is anticipated to feature Federal Reserve rate cuts or a plateau, following peak rates in 2023. The provided known value (1.680 as of late Oct 2025) is below the peak figures from previous years, reflecting a gradual downward trend or stability. National deposit rates react sluggishly but typically trend downwards in such cycles. Since a strict increase is required for resolution, a 'flat' outcome results in a NO. Given the sluggishness of national deposit rates, the probability of a strict aggregate increase in just 1 month (Nov to Dec) is small (estimated 22%). Over a 3-month horizon (Nov to Feb), there is somewhat larger variance, slightly increasing the likelihood of a random upward tick or temporary fluctuation (estimated 32%), but the broader downward expected trend keeps both probabilities significantly below 50% and below the empirical FRED base rate of 42.2%.
